title: Branded 404 Error Page with Redirect
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to see a branded, user-friendly page when I navigate to an invalid URL, so that I can easily find my way back to the application instead of seeing a raw server error.
description:
When users or bots navigate to URLs that do not exist (e.g., old URLs like `/apps/login.php`), the application should capture the request and display a branded 404 page instead of the default server error.

## Requirements

- Create a `404.php` (or equivalent) error page with the DataTools Pro branding.
- Include the application header and footer for consistent look.
- Display a friendly message explaining the URL does not exist.
- Provide links to the DataTools App and DataTools Support.
- Configure `.htaccess` (or Laravel error handling) to route all 404 errors to this page.

## Design

The page should include branded header/footer elements, a clear message that the page was not found, and navigation links back to the main application.
acceptance_criteria: Navigating to a non-existent URL displays a branded 404 page
The 404 page includes the DataTools Pro header and footer
The page displays a user-friendly message instead of raw error codes
Links to the main app and support are provided
The .htaccess or Laravel route configuration correctly catches all 404 errors
ux_changes: New branded 404 error page with DataTools Pro header, footer, friendly messaging, and navigation links to the main app and support.
middleware_changes: Laravel/Apache error handling configured to route all 404 responses to the custom error page.
implementation_notes: Initial implementation via .htaccess required the hosting provider to set AllowOverride to All. The 404 page was designed with branded elements and went through design review to add header/footer. Product owner requested a branded look rather than a generic error page.
comment_summary: Developer created a 404.php file and added .htaccess rules but it wasn't working due to server AllowOverride settings. A hosting provider ticket was opened to resolve. The initial design was updated to include branded header and footer per product owner request.
